cast_and_dedupe.description: Casts source columns to target types and deduplicates rows on a key, keeping the latest row per an ordering column.

params_schema.source_table: string - fully qualified object_id of the single upstream source
params_schema.key_cols: list[string] - columns forming the dedupe key (usually the target's grain)
params_schema.order_col: string - column used to pick the 'latest' row when deduping
params_schema.cast_map: object - {column_name: target_type} for columns that need a type cast

aggregate.description: Groups a single source by group_by_cols and computes one aggregate per output column via agg_map. No computed expressions - each aggregate is a single Spark agg function over a single existing source column (or a bare count).

params_schema.source_table: string - fully qualified object_id of the single upstream source
params_schema.group_by_cols: list[string] - columns to group by (usually the target's grain)
params_schema.agg_map: object - {output_column: {source_col: string|null, func: 'sum'|'count'|'avg'|'min'|'max'}}. source_col is null only when func is 'count' (row count).

cast_dedupe_join.description: Casts + dedupes a primary source (same as cast_and_dedupe), then left-joins in a secondary source (typically the output of an aggregate template) on a shared key column.

params_schema.primary_source: string - fully qualified object_id of the primary (dedupe-then-cast) source
params_schema.primary_key_cols: list[string] - dedupe key on the primary source
params_schema.primary_order_col: string - column used to pick the 'latest' row when deduping the primary source
params_schema.primary_cast_map: object - {column_name: target_type} for columns on the primary source that need a cast
params_schema.join_source: string - fully qualified object_id of the secondary source to left-join in
params_schema.join_key: string - column name to join on; must exist, with the same name, in both sources and in the target's own columns
